response.justification: The candidate is a highly capable senior/staff engineer with nearly two decades of experience, but this creates a fundamental mismatch with an entry-level junior position — both in compensation expectations and retention risk. More critically, the role requires strong verbal and written German, while the resume lists German as only basic, and Java appears as a secondary skill with little recent professional use compared to his TypeScript/Node.js and DLang focus.
strengths: Deep software craftsmanship background (TDD, pairing, CI/CD, trunk-based development) aligned with agile project work
Broad full-stack and systems experience across web, mobile, embedded, and cloud environments
Completed computer science degree and already based in Germany (Berlin)
concerns: Severely overqualified for an entry-level role, posing salary-mismatch and retention risks
German listed as basic, while the role explicitly requires strong German communication with clients
Java is not his primary language — recent professional work centers on TypeScript/Node.js and DLang
